title: B.Sc. (Hons with Research) Forensic Science with specialization in Forensic Toxicology and Explosives
content: The B.Sc. (Hons. with Research) Forensic Science with specialization in Forensic Toxicology and Explosives is a comprehensive program designed for students seeking expertise in the detection and analysis of toxic substances and explosive materials. This research-oriented course combines strong theoretical foundations with intensive hands-on training in state-of-the-art laboratories equipped for chemical analysis, toxicological screening, and explosive residue examination. Students gain practical experience in identifying poisons, drugs, chemical agents, and explosives through advanced instrumentation and simulated forensic casework. The program emphasizes experiential learning, analytical precision, and the legal aspects of handling hazardous substances, while also encouraging students to undertake independent research projects in their final year. Graduates are well-prepared for careers in forensic science laboratories, anti-terrorism units, law enforcement agencies, environmental monitoring organizations, pharmaceutical industries, and chemical analysis firms. The program also provides a solid foundation for pursuing postgraduate studies and research in forensic toxicology, analytical chemistry, or explosives science. As the need for skilled professionals in crime detection, public safety, and homeland security grows, this specialization equips students with the scientific knowledge and research capabilities to address complex challenges in both criminal and environmental investigations. Students with a CUET / SAT (DI Code - 71057 ) score will be given preference in the merit list 1) B.Sc (Hons/Hons with Research) in Forensic Science Ist yr. with minimum 50% marks or 5.0 CGPA on a 10 point scale 2) XII with Science with minimum 50% marks. 01 Core Chemical Forensics Foundation: Provide rigorous training in analytical chemistry techniques (spectroscopy, chromatography, mass spectrometry) applied to forensic evidence analysis including drugs, poisons, explosives and trace materials. 02 Toxicology Specialization: Develop expertise in postmortem toxicology, drug metabolism, poison detection and interpretation of toxicological findings in legal contexts using modern laboratory instrumentation. 03 Forensic Evidence Processing: Train students in standard operating procedures for chemical evidence collection, preservation, and analysis while maintaining chain-of-custody protocols for courtroom admissibility. 04 Research & Method Development : Cultivate research skills through projects on novel detection methods, designer drug analysis, environmental toxicology and emerging challenges in chemical forensics. 05 Industry & Regulatory Preparedness: Prepare graduates for careers in crime labs, medical examiner offices, pharmaceutical companies and regulatory agencies through internships and training aligned with SWGDRUG and SOFT/AAFS standards. 01 02 03 04 05 06 07 08 01 02 03 01 02 03 Students with a CUET / SAT (DI Code - 71057 ) score will be given preference in the merit list 1) B.Sc (Hons/Hons with Research) in Forensic Science Ist yr. with minimum 50% marks or 5.0 CGPA on a 10 point scale 2) XII with Science with minimum 50% marks.
